name: Windows Server Monitoring
author: OpsDB Platform
description: Monitor Windows Server VMs and physical hosts via WinRM: CPU, memory, disk, network, services, processes, event log, and security events. Provides real-time performance counters, critical service health, event log error tracking, and security audit monitoring.

setup_requirements.title: WinRM Service Enabled
setup_requirements.description: Windows Remote Management must be running and listening on the configured port (default 5985).

setup_requirements.title: NTLM Authentication Enabled
setup_requirements.description: NTLM transport requires WSMan NTLM auth to be active. Required when auth_transport=ntlm.

setup_requirements.title: Basic Authentication Enabled (for local accounts)
setup_requirements.description: Basic transport requires WSMan Basic auth and AllowUnencrypted when using HTTP. Must be run as Administrator in PowerShell, followed by a WinRM service restart.

setup_requirements.title: Firewall Port 5985 Open (HTTP)
setup_requirements.description: Windows Firewall must allow inbound TCP 5985 for WinRM HTTP. Use 5986 for HTTPS.
fix_commands: netsh advfirewall firewall add rule name="WinRM HTTP" protocol=TCP dir=in localport=5985 action=allow

setup_requirements.title: PowerShell 4.0 or Later
setup_requirements.description: All collectors require PowerShell 4.0+ for CIM cmdlets, Get-Counter, Get-WinEvent, and ConvertTo-Json with depth support.

setup_requirements.title: LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy (for non-domain setups)
setup_requirements.description: When connecting with a local Administrator account, UAC token filtering may block remote WinRM access. Setting this policy allows local admins to authenticate remotely.

win.system_uptime.description: Server uptime in seconds, last boot time, OS caption, build number, and hostname. Run every minute to detect unexpected reboots.

win.cpu_utilization.description: Total CPU usage percentage (real-time performance counter), processor queue length, and physical/logical core counts.
win.cpu_utilization.command: $cpu=[math]::Round((Get-Counter '\Processor(_Total)\% Processor Time').CounterSamples[0].CookedValue,1);$queue=[int](Get-Counter '\System\Processor Queue Length' -EA SilentlyContinue).CounterSamples[0].CookedValue;$p=Get-CimInstance Win32_Processor;$cores=($p|Measure-Object NumberOfCores -Sum).Sum;$lp=($p|Measure-Object NumberOfLogicalProcessors -Sum).Sum;@{cpu_usage_percent=$cpu;processor_queue_length=$queue;physical_cores=[int]$cores;logical_processors=[int]$lp}|ConvertTo-Json -Compress

win.memory_utilization.description: Memory usage percentage, free/used/total bytes, page faults per second, and committed memory percentage.

win.disk_utilization.description: Aggregate disk usage across all volumes: drive count, maximum used percentage, total/free/used bytes. Use win.disk_details for per-drive breakdown.

win.disk_details.description: Per-volume disk space breakdown: drive letter, used/free/total bytes, and usage percentage. Returns one row per mounted volume.

win.disk_io.description: Physical disk I/O throughput and latency: read/write bytes per second and average read/write latency in milliseconds (all physical disks combined).
win.disk_io.command: $c=Get-Counter '\PhysicalDisk(_Total)\Disk Read Bytes/sec','\PhysicalDisk(_Total)\Disk Write Bytes/sec','\PhysicalDisk(_Total)\Avg. Disk sec/Read','\PhysicalDisk(_Total)\Avg. Disk sec/Write' -EA SilentlyContinue;$s=$c.CounterSamples;$rbps=($s|?{$_.Path -match 'Read Bytes'})[0].CookedValue;$wbps=($s|?{$_.Path -match 'Write Bytes'})[0].CookedValue;$rms=($s|?{$_.Path -match 'sec/Read'})[0].CookedValue;$wms=($s|?{$_.Path -match 'sec/Write'})[0].CookedValue;@{disk_read_bytes_per_sec=[math]::Round($rbps);disk_write_bytes_per_sec=[math]::Round($wbps);avg_disk_read_ms=[math]::Round($rms*1000,2);avg_disk_write_ms=[math]::Round($wms*1000,2)}|ConvertTo-Json -Compress

win.network_io.description: Aggregate network throughput and error counts across all active interfaces: bytes received/sent per second, and packet receive/send error counts.
win.network_io.command: $c=Get-Counter '\Network Interface(*)\Bytes Received/sec','\Network Interface(*)\Bytes Sent/sec','\Network Interface(*)\Packets Received Errors','\Network Interface(*)\Packets Outbound Errors' -EA SilentlyContinue;$s=$c.CounterSamples;@{total_bytes_received_per_sec=[math]::Round(($s|?{$_.Path -match 'Received/sec'}|Measure-Object CookedValue -Sum).Sum);total_bytes_sent_per_sec=[math]::Round(($s|?{$_.Path -match 'Sent/sec'}|Measure-Object CookedValue -Sum).Sum);total_receive_errors=[long]($s|?{$_.Path -match 'Received Errors'}|Measure-Object CookedValue -Sum).Sum;total_send_errors=[long]($s|?{$_.Path -match 'Outbound Errors'}|Measure-Object CookedValue -Sum).Sum}|ConvertTo-Json -Compress

win.network_adapters.description: Per-adapter network statistics for all UP adapters: name, link speed, cumulative bytes received/sent, and receive packet errors.

win.tcp_connections.description: TCP connection state counts: established, time-wait, close-wait, listening ports, and total connections.

win.services_health.description: Service health summary: total, running, and stopped service counts, plus count of critical system services that are not running.

win.services_details.description: All non-disabled services with their current status and start type. Returns one row per service — use to investigate which services are stopped.

win.event_log_errors.description: Error and Critical event counts from System and Application logs in the past hour, split by log and severity level.

win.event_log_details.description: Most recent 50 Error and Critical events from System and Application logs in the past 2 hours. Returns rows with timestamp, source, level, event ID, and message excerpt.

win.process_top_cpu.description: Top 20 processes by cumulative CPU time: process name, PID, CPU seconds consumed since start, working set memory in MB, thread count, and handle count.

win.process_top_memory.description: Top 20 processes by working set memory usage: process name, PID, working set MB, private memory MB, handle count, and thread count.

win.performance_overview.description: System-wide process, thread, and handle counts from performance counters. Useful for detecting handle or thread leaks.

win.security_events.description: Security audit event counts from the Security log in the past hour: failed logon attempts (Event 4625), account lockouts (Event 4740), and explicit credential logon attempts (Event 4648).

win.local_users.description: Local user account summary: total, enabled, and disabled local accounts, and number of members in the local Administrators group.

win.system_info.description: OS edition, version, build, architecture, domain membership, total memory, processor count, installed patch count, days since last patch, and pending reboot status.

win_cpu_usage_warning.title: CPU Usage High (Warning)
win_cpu_usage_warning.recommendation: Review running services and processes. Use win.process_top_cpu to identify high-CPU processes. Check for runaway scheduled tasks or AV scans.

win_cpu_usage_critical.title: CPU Usage Critical (90%+)
win_cpu_usage_critical.recommendation: Server is CPU-saturated. Identify the top-consuming processes immediately. Consider terminating runaway processes or migrating workloads.

win_memory_usage_warning.title: Memory Usage High (Warning)
win_memory_usage_warning.recommendation: Memory usage exceeds 80%. Review top memory consumers with win.process_top_memory. Check for memory leaks in long-running services.

win_memory_usage_critical.title: Memory Usage Critical (90%+)
win_memory_usage_critical.recommendation: Memory is nearly exhausted. Windows will begin aggressive paging, causing severe performance degradation. Identify and restart or kill the largest memory consumers immediately.

win_processor_queue_high.title: Processor Queue Length High
win_processor_queue_high.recommendation: More than 10 threads are queued waiting for CPU time. The server is CPU-bound. Reduce workload or add CPU capacity. A sustained queue > 2 per core indicates saturation.

win_page_faults_high.title: Page Faults Rate High
win_page_faults_high.recommendation: High page fault rate indicates memory pressure. Soft faults (page already in memory) are benign; hard faults (disk reads) severely impact performance. Monitor committed_pct and reduce memory pressure.

win_committed_memory_high.title: Committed Memory Usage Critical
win_committed_memory_high.recommendation: Virtual memory commitment exceeds 90% of the commit limit (RAM + page file). Risk of out-of-memory conditions. Increase page file size or add RAM. Identify memory-consuming processes immediately.

win_disk_space_warning.title: Disk Space Usage High (Warning)
win_disk_space_warning.recommendation: At least one volume is over 80% full. Review win.disk_details to identify which drive. Run Disk Cleanup, archive old files, and check Windows Update cache (WinSxS).

win_disk_space_critical.title: Disk Space Usage Critical (90%+)
win_disk_space_critical.recommendation: A volume is over 90% full. Windows services may fail or produce errors when disk space is exhausted. Immediately free space or expand the volume.

win_disk_read_latency_high.title: Disk Read Latency High (>20 ms)
win_disk_read_latency_high.recommendation: Average disk read latency exceeds 20 ms. Acceptable for HDD but concerning for SSD/NVMe. Check for disk fragmentation, failing drives, or storage contention. Consider enabling Write Caching.

win_disk_write_latency_high.title: Disk Write Latency High (>20 ms)
win_disk_write_latency_high.recommendation: Average disk write latency exceeds 20 ms. Review I/O patterns and check for disk health issues using SMART data. Check for antivirus real-time scanning on busy directories.

win_network_errors_warning.title: Network Receive Errors Detected
win_network_errors_warning.recommendation: Network adapter receive errors are accumulating. Check cable connections, NIC driver, and duplex settings. Run Get-NetAdapterStatistics to identify the affected adapter.

win_network_errors_critical.title: Network Receive Errors Critical
win_network_errors_critical.recommendation: High network error rate indicates a hardware or driver problem. Replace the cable, check the switch port, or update the NIC driver. This may cause packet loss affecting all services.

win_critical_service_stopped.title: Critical Windows Service Stopped
win_critical_service_stopped.recommendation: One or more critical Windows services (WMI, Event Log, RPC, DHCP, etc.) are not running. This can cause cascading failures. Check Services (services.msc) and Event Log for the stop reason.

win_services_stopped_many.title: Many Services Stopped
win_services_stopped_many.recommendation: More than 10 non-disabled services are in Stopped state. Review win.services_details to identify which services have stopped unexpectedly. Check Event Log for crash or dependency failure events.

win_event_errors_warning.title: Event Log Error Rate High
win_event_errors_warning.recommendation: More than 50 Error-level events in the past hour. Review win.event_log_details to identify the sources. Common causes: application crashes, driver errors, disk warnings, or service failures.

win_event_errors_critical.title: Event Log Error Rate Critical
win_event_errors_critical.recommendation: Over 200 errors in the past hour indicates a systemic problem. Review Event Viewer immediately for the dominant error source. Consider whether an impending hardware failure or application loop is the cause.

win_critical_events_detected.title: Critical-Level Events in Event Log
win_critical_events_detected.recommendation: Critical-level events (Level 1) have been recorded. These represent the most severe system issues. Review win.event_log_details filtering by level 'Critical' and investigate immediately.

win_failed_logons_warning.title: Failed Logon Attempts Elevated
win_failed_logons_warning.recommendation: More than 20 failed logon attempts in the past hour (Event 4625). May indicate brute-force attempts or misconfigured services. Review the source IPs and accounts in Event Viewer Security log.

win_failed_logons_critical.title: Failed Logon Attempts Critical — Possible Brute-Force
win_failed_logons_critical.recommendation: Over 100 failed logons in an hour is a strong indicator of a brute-force attack. Block the source IP at the firewall level, enable account lockout policy, and verify no accounts have been compromised.

win_account_lockouts_detected.title: Account Lockout Detected
win_account_lockouts_detected.recommendation: One or more accounts were locked out in the past hour (Event 4740). This may indicate a brute-force attack or a stale cached credential from a service or scheduled task. Investigate the source workstation and account.

win_admin_accounts_high.title: Excessive Local Administrator Accounts
win_admin_accounts_high.recommendation: More than 5 accounts are members of the local Administrators group. Follow the principle of least privilege. Remove unnecessary admin memberships and use dedicated service accounts with minimal permissions.

win_pending_reboot.title: System Restart Pending
win_pending_reboot.recommendation: Windows has flagged that a restart is required (likely for a Windows Update or driver installation). Schedule a maintenance window to reboot. Running with pending updates may leave security patches unapplied.

win_patches_outdated.title: System Not Patched for 90+ Days
win_patches_outdated.recommendation: No Windows updates have been installed in over 90 days. This server may be missing critical security patches. Run Windows Update immediately or investigate why automatic updates are not applying.

hints: What is the current CPU utilization trend and are there any processes causing CPU spikes?
Is the processor queue length a concern for this server's workload?
Which processes are consuming the most CPU time right now?
Are there any signs of CPU throttling or power management affecting performance?
Should this server's workload be redistributed to other hosts?
Is the memory usage within acceptable bounds, or is the server under memory pressure?
Which processes are consuming the most memory and could any be restarted to reclaim it?
Are any disk volumes approaching capacity? Which one needs attention first?
Is disk I/O latency within acceptable thresholds for the storage type (HDD vs SSD)?
Are there signs of excessive paging or virtual memory pressure?
Are any critical Windows services stopped or in an unexpected state?
What is the server uptime and were there any recent unexpected reboots?
Are there any services that have failed and may need to be restarted?
Is the TCP connection profile normal or are there signs of connection leaks or port exhaustion?
Are there any critical or error events in the Windows Event Log that need immediate attention?
What are the dominant event sources generating the most errors?
Are there signs of a brute-force attack or unusual failed logon patterns?
Are the local Administrator group members appropriate, or should any be removed?
Is this server patched and up-to-date? Does it have a pending reboot for updates?
Provide an executive summary of this Windows server's overall health.
What are the top 3 most urgent issues to address on this server right now?
What proactive maintenance tasks would improve the stability of this server over the next 30 days?
Based on the findings and metrics, what is the overall risk level for this server?

metrics.name: cpu_usage_percent
metrics.label: CPU Usage
metrics.hint: Total CPU utilization across all cores

metrics.name: processor_queue_length
metrics.label: Processor Queue
metrics.hint: Threads waiting for CPU; sustained > 2x core count indicates saturation

metrics.name: uptime_seconds
metrics.label: Uptime
metrics.hint: Seconds since last boot. Low value indicates a recent reboot.

metrics.name: total_processes
metrics.label: Total Processes
metrics.hint: All running processes on the system

metrics.name: total_threads
metrics.label: Total Threads
metrics.hint: All threads; rising value may indicate a thread leak

metrics.name: memory_used_percent
metrics.label: Memory Used
metrics.hint: Physical RAM in use as a percentage

metrics.name: committed_pct
metrics.label: Commit Charge
metrics.hint: Virtual memory committed vs commit limit; > 80% risks out-of-memory

metrics.name: page_faults_per_sec
metrics.label: Page Faults/s
metrics.hint: Hard page faults per second; high value indicates memory pressure

metrics.name: memory_free_bytes
metrics.label: Free Memory
metrics.hint: Physical RAM currently free

metrics.name: memory_total_bytes
metrics.label: Total Memory
metrics.hint: Total installed physical RAM

metrics.name: max_disk_used_pct
metrics.label: Max Disk Used
metrics.hint: Highest used % across all drives

metrics.name: total_disk_bytes
metrics.label: Total Disk
metrics.hint: Combined capacity of all volumes

metrics.name: free_disk_bytes
metrics.label: Free Disk
metrics.hint: Combined free space across all volumes

metrics.name: avg_disk_read_ms
metrics.label: Avg Disk Read
metrics.hint: Average disk read latency; > 20 ms on HDDs or > 2 ms on SSDs warrants investigation

metrics.name: avg_disk_write_ms
metrics.label: Avg Disk Write
metrics.hint: Average disk write latency

metrics.name: disk_read_bytes_per_sec
metrics.label: Disk Read B/s
metrics.hint: Physical disk read throughput

metrics.name: disk_write_bytes_per_sec
metrics.label: Disk Write B/s
metrics.hint: Physical disk write throughput

metrics.name: total_bytes_received_per_sec
metrics.label: Bytes In/s
metrics.hint: Total inbound network throughput across all adapters

metrics.name: total_bytes_sent_per_sec
metrics.label: Bytes Out/s
metrics.hint: Total outbound network throughput

metrics.name: total_receive_errors
metrics.label: Receive Errors
metrics.hint: Cumulative inbound packet errors — non-zero warrants NIC investigation

metrics.name: total_send_errors
metrics.label: Send Errors
metrics.hint: Cumulative outbound packet errors

metrics.name: established_connections
metrics.label: Established Connections
metrics.hint: TCP connections in ESTABLISHED state

metrics.name: time_wait_connections
metrics.label: TIME_WAIT Connections
metrics.hint: TCP connections in TIME_WAIT; high count may indicate port exhaustion

metrics.name: critical_services_stopped
metrics.label: Critical Services Stopped
metrics.hint: Count of critical services not running

metrics.name: stopped_services
metrics.label: Stopped Services
metrics.hint: Total services in stopped state

metrics.name: running_services
metrics.label: Running Services
metrics.hint: Total services currently running

metrics.name: event_error_count
metrics.label: Event Errors
metrics.hint: Error events in System/Application logs in last collection window

metrics.name: critical_event_count
metrics.label: Critical Events
metrics.hint: Critical-level events in Windows Event Log

metrics.name: failed_logon_count
metrics.label: Failed Logons/hr
metrics.hint: Event 4625 count — > 20/hr indicates brute-force or misconfigured credentials

metrics.name: account_lockout_count
metrics.label: Account Lockouts
metrics.hint: Event 4740 count

metrics.name: admin_group_members
metrics.label: Admin Group Members
metrics.hint: Local Administrators group member count; > 5 is flagged as risk

metrics.name: explicit_credential_logons
metrics.label: Explicit Credential Logons
metrics.hint: Event 4648 — may indicate pass-the-hash or credential reuse

metrics.name: enabled_users
metrics.label: Enabled Local Users
metrics.hint: Count of enabled local user accounts

metrics.name: total_local_users
metrics.label: Total Local Users
metrics.hint: All local user accounts including disabled

metrics.name: pending_reboot
metrics.label: Pending Reboot
metrics.hint: 1 = reboot required to apply patches; 0 = clean

metrics.name: last_patch_days_ago
metrics.label: Days Since Last Patch
metrics.hint: Days since last Windows Update installation; > 60 warrants investigation

metrics.name: installed_patches
metrics.label: Installed Patches
metrics.hint: Total cumulative patches installed
